title: inula paludosa
intro: Inula paludosa, also known as Marsh Elecampane, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Inula paludosa include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Inula paludosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its root is also used in herbal medicine to make capsules and tablets.
